page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations fall within the average tier. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 565.97 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weaker, placing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, while the instance handles small-model LLM inference adequately, performance degrades significantly on medium 7B models, with text generation falling into the bottom 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the c3d-standard-8 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core instances. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid single-core performance, this instance is well-suited for web serving, text processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, workloads requiring high memory bandwidth for large datasets or intensive machine learning inference may encounter performance bottlenecks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 physical cores with multithreading and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, though the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. However, uncached memory bandwidth and medium-model LLM inference speeds are weak. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and text processing, offering a balanced resource profile for applications that do not require massive memory bandwidth or local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-8 is an x86_64 VM with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC CPU for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores and 32 GB of memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM.
Features dedicated CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and multithreading.
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance.
Includes AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications.
